narration_text: Now we'll see how nodal processing works in the context of network delays. Notice that the time it takes for a packet to be processed at a node is typically very short, less than a microsecond. This processing involves checking for bit errors and determining the output link. Look at the diagram on your slide — notice how the queueing delay is represented, which is the time a packet waits at the output link for transmission. This expression essentially tells us how the total delay at a node is calculated, taking into account processing, queueing, transmission, and propagation delays. I want you to focus on how these components interact to affect overall network performance, which we'll explore further in the next section.
